name: Akira Kurosawa
biography: Master of Japanese cinema known for epic samurai films and psychological dramas.

name: Federico Fellini
biography: Pioneer of Italian neorealism and later art-house cinema, celebrated for dreamlike narratives.

name: Ingmar Bergman
biography: Renowned for existential and psychological explorations of the human condition.

name: Stanley Kubrick
biography: Perfectionist auteur spanning horror, sci-fi, and historical drama with meticulous visual style.

name: Jean-Luc Godard
biography: Founding figure of the French New Wave, known for radical narrative and editorial experimentation.

name: François Truffaut
biography: Key New Wave director whose semi-autobiographical works redefined coming-of-age cinema.

name: Orson Welles
biography: Innovative filmmaker whose Citizen Kane revolutionised cinematography and narrative structure.

name: Alfred Hitchcock
biography: Master of suspense whose psychological thrillers set the template for modern genre filmmaking.

name: Francis Ford Coppola
biography: Director of The Godfather trilogy and Apocalypse Now, defining American New Hollywood cinema.

name: Martin Scorsese
biography: New York filmmaker celebrated for crime dramas, documentary work, and film preservation advocacy.

name: Andrei Tarkovsky
biography: Soviet auteur known for deeply meditative, visually poetic explorations of memory and spirituality.

name: Wong Kar-wai
biography: Celebrated for lyrical, fragmented storytelling and saturated visual style in Hong Kong cinema.

name: Pedro Almodóvar
biography: Spanish auteur known for melodramatic, colourful explorations of identity, desire, and family.

name: Abbas Kiarostami
biography: Iranian master whose minimalist approach blurred the line between documentary and fiction.

name: Terrence Malick
biography: Philosophical filmmaker known for sparse dialogue and transcendent, nature-infused imagery.

name: David Lynch
biography: Surrealist auteur whose films explore the disturbing undercurrents of American suburban life.

name: Agnès Varda
biography: Pioneer of the French New Wave and feminist cinema, celebrated for playful documentary hybrids.

name: Ousmane Sembène
biography: Father of African cinema, whose films gave voice to post-colonial West African experience.

name: Chantal Akerman
biography: Minimalist filmmaker whose Jeanne Dielman redefined feminist and structural cinema.

name: Satyajit Ray
biography: Bengali auteur whose Apu Trilogy brought Indian cinema to international acclaim.
